contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert Wh to mAh?
contentBlocks.html:
Watt-hours (Wh) and milliamp-hours (mAh) both measure battery capacity, but at different scales. The relationship depends on voltage: mAh = Wh × 1000 / V.

Phone batteries are typically rated in mAh (e.g., 5,000 mAh at 3.7V = 18.5 Wh). Laptop batteries and power banks often use Wh (e.g., 100 Wh is the airline carry-on limit).

This conversion is essential for comparing battery capacities, checking airline power bank limits, and sizing solar/UPS systems.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse

contentBlocks.title: How does Wh to mAh conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
Energy (Wh) = Capacity (Ah) × Voltage (V). Therefore: mAh = Wh × 1,000 / V, and Wh = mAh × V / 1,000.

Example: 100 Wh battery at 3.7V = 100 × 1,000 / 3.7 = 27,027 mAh.

The voltage matters! The same Wh rating gives different mAh at different voltages. Always specify voltage for accurate conversion.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Airline limit: 100 Wh. At 3.7V = 27,027 mAh. Most 20,000 mAh power banks = 74 Wh (allowed).
  • Phone battery: 5,000 mAh × 3.7V = 18.5 Wh.
  • Laptop: MacBook Air = 52.6 Wh. MacBook Pro 16" = 99.6 Wh.
  • 18650 cell: 3,000 mAh × 3.7V = 11.1 Wh. Tesla uses thousands of these.

items.question: How do I convert Wh to mAh?
items.answer: mAh = Wh × 1,000 / Voltage. Example: 100 Wh at 3.7V = 27,027 mAh.

items.question: What is the airline power bank limit?
items.answer: Most airlines allow power banks up to 100 Wh (some allow 160 Wh with approval). At 3.7V, 100 Wh = ~27,000 mAh.

items.question: Is mAh or Wh better for comparing batteries?
items.answer: Wh is more accurate because it accounts for voltage. Two 5,000 mAh batteries at different voltages have different energy.

items.question: How many mAh is a laptop battery?
items.answer: Depends on voltage. A 50 Wh laptop battery at 11.4V = 4,386 mAh. At 7.6V = 6,579 mAh.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
